Her paintings were simplified portrayals of nature, focusing on things like the Western desert, cows' skulls, flowers, rocks, hills, and the sky. She moved to New Mexico permanently after her husband's death because she was inspired by the landscape there. She continued to paint these natural elements throughout her life.
